title: JARVIS Step 36 — Optional Browser TTS Answer Readback
purpose: Add optional browser text-to-speech so JARVIS can read the returned answer aloud after a successful read-only ask response. TTS is off by default, manually controlled (click-gated), and never auto-speaks. It reads only the last rendered answer (or refusal explanation), never the question or any command/action/execute field, makes no network call, and stores nothing.
relation_to_steps_20_35: Step 30 the answer-only POST /api/jarvis/ask; Step 31 the read-only ask UI; Step 33 the docs-only voice-layer safety plan; Step 34 the disabled voice preview; Step 35 browser speech-to-text that fills the input only. Step 36 implements the final voice roadmap item from Step 33: optional browser SpeechSynthesis that reads returned answers aloud, off by default, with no backend, no storage, and no auto-speak.
files_changed: templates/jarvis.html — added an optional Voice output block inside the existing #jvVoicePreview area: a non-interactive Read last answer plus a status line. Added a module-level jvLastAnswerText, set inside renderAsk ONLY from the model answer (d.answer) or refusal explanation (d.refusal_reason) — reset on every response. Added wireSpeak(): it uses window.speechSynthesis + SpeechSynthesisUtterance, is off by default, speaks only on explicit click/Enter/Space, and shows 'Voice output unavailable in this browser.' when unsupported. No auto-speak, no fetch, no storage. STT and the {question}-only ask flow are unchanged.

tests/test_jarvis_route.py — reconciled two prior tests (Step 34/35 audio scans) that forbade speechSynthesis, since Step 36 intentionally adds browser TTS (kept getUserMedia/MediaRecorder/AudioContext/mediaDevices forbidden); added a Step 36 section (16 tests).
prior_tests_reconciled: test_jarvis_step_34_no_microphone_or_audio_apis — dropped 'speechsynthesis' from the forbidden list (Step 36 adds browser TTS); still forbids getUserMedia/MediaRecorder/AudioContext/navigator.mediaDevices.

test_jarvis_step_35_no_forbidden_audio_or_tts_apis — dropped 'speechsynthesis' from the forbidden list (Step 36 adds browser TTS); still forbids getUserMedia/MediaRecorder/AudioContext/navigator.mediaDevices.
explicitly_not_done: No external TTS API; no fetch/XHR from the read-aloud handler.
No audio recording or storage; no transcript storage.
No auto-speak: the response renderer never speaks; speech is click-gated only.
Read-aloud never speaks the question or any command/action/execute field; for a refusal it speaks only the refusal explanation.
No change to STT: voice still fills the input only and does not auto-submit.
No refresh / execution / broker / paper / live control.
No change to the /api/jarvis/ask payload — still {question} only.

tts_answer_readback_summary.location: Inside the existing #jvVoicePreview block in the 'Talk to JARVIS' panel, below the speech-to-text row.
labels: Read answer aloud (READ ANSWER ALOUD tag)
tts_answer_readback_summary.control: Read last answer — disabled until an answer exists; wired only with addEventListener (click + Enter/Space); never a real button/form/submit.
tts_answer_readback_summary.mechanism: window.speechSynthesis + new SpeechSynthesisUtterance(text); lang=en-US, rate=1. synth.cancel() before each utterance.
tts_answer_readback_summary.source_text: jvLastAnswerText, set in renderAsk ONLY from String(d.answer), else (refused) String(d.refusal_reason). Reset to '' on every response; never the question; never command/action/execute fields.
tts_answer_readback_summary.off_by_default: The control starts disabled and never speaks until the operator clicks it after an answer renders. The ask response renderer makes no speak call.
tts_answer_readback_summary.graceful_degradation: If window.speechSynthesis or SpeechSynthesisUtterance is unavailable, the control disables and shows 'Voice output unavailable in this browser.'
tts_answer_readback_summary.stt_unchanged: wireVoice() still fills #jvAskInput only and does not auto-submit or fetch.
tts_answer_readback_summary.ask_flow: Unchanged: #jvAskInput + 'Ask read-only' still POST only {question}; the classifier decides.
tts_answer_readback_summary.persistent_warning: 'JARVIS answers only. No execution. No trading control.' retained.

recommended_next_safe_step: Step 37 (JARVIS-VOICE-LIVE-SMOKE, separate approval): a live end-to-end voice smoke — speak -> transcript fills input -> click Ask read-only -> answer/refusal -> optional click Read last answer. Validation/report-only; spoken safe + forbidden questions behave identically to typed, with no storage, no refresh, no execution, and no auto-speak.
conclusion: JARVIS can now optionally read a returned answer aloud, but only when the operator clicks the off-by-default 'Read last answer' control. It uses the browser SpeechSynthesis API exclusively, reads only the last answer (or refusal explanation), never the question or any command, never auto-speaks, makes no network call, and stores nothing. STT still fills the input only; the {question}-only payload, the persistent warning, and the /api/jarvis/status shape are unchanged. app.py and jarvis_conversation_safety.py are untouched. Nothing staged or committed.
